properties.remark: CORRECTS PREVIOUS NON-TSTM WND GST REPORT FROM 1 SSE SPRINGFIELD. ASOS STATION KBTL BATTLE CREEK EXECUTIVE AIRPORT.
TREES AND WIRES DOWN WITH POWER OUTAGE. VINYL SIDING PEELED OFF HOUSE. SMALL SECTION OF ROOF BLOWN OFF HOUSE. NEAR 92ND ST AND EASTERN AVE.

A VERY SMALL, BRIEF, AND WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE PRESERVATION LAKES SUBDIVISION IN SOUTHERN KENT COUNTY, CAUSING DAMAGE TO 6 TO 12 HOMES. PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT 1/2 MILE, WIDTH 65 YARDS, AND IT WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ONLY ABOUT ONE MINUTE. THE MOST NOTABLE DAMAGE WAS A SMALL PORTION OF A ROOF TORN OFF ONE HOME, SOME SIDING STRIPPED OFF TWO HOMES, AND A PARTIALLY PUSHED IN GARAGE DOOR. SECURITY/DOORBELL VIDEO REVEALED THAT OBJECTS WERE LOFTED AND CARRIED THROUGH THE AIR. AT LEAST TWO VEHICLES SUSTAINED DAMAGE FROM BEING STRUCK BY FLYING DEBRIS SUCH AS LAWN CHAIRS.

LARGE TREE DOWN.

EF-0 BROKEN 2.4 MILE PATH OF DAMAGE TO SMALL TO MEDIUM TREE LIMBS OR SMALL GROUPS OF TREES UPROOTED. ALONG FILLMORE ST WEST OF 104TH A STORAGE SHED OUTBUILDING HAD A GARAGE DOOR BLOWN IN WHICH CAUSED SMALL SECTION OF ROOF TO LIFT OFF AND TREES NEARBY WERE UPROOTED. EVIDENCE OF DEBRIS LOFTING ON THIS PROPERTY. A CAMPER TRAILER ON ANOTHER PROPERTY TO THE NORTHEAST WAS FLIPPED BUT NO DAMAGE WAS SUSTAINED AT A NEARBY TURKEY BARN.
CAMPER BACK TIPPED OVER. TREE LIMBS DOWN.
EF-1 WITH PATH LENGTH ABOUT 3 MILES. WOOD BARN HEAVILY DAMAGED. CENTER PIVOT IRRIGATION TIPPED OVER. OPEN AIR ANIMAL BARN SHEET METAL ROOF TORN APART AND POWER POLES SNAPPED DOWNWIND.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN AND UPROOTED.

EF-0 WITH PATH LENGTH 0.6 MILES. TREES AND LIMBS WERE SNAPPED FROM BENNETT ST AND CUMBERLAND AVE TO VERGENNES ST EAST OF CUMBERLAND AVE.
EF-0 WITH PATH LENGTH OF ABOUT 1 MILE. BARNS AND TOPS OF SILOS WERE DAMAGED ALONG VEDDER ROAD EAST OF HASTINGS ROAD.

EF-1 WITH PATH LENGTH ABOUT 4.5 MILES. ONE HOME WITH ATTACHED GARAGE ADDITION HAD HALF OF THE ROOF TORN OFF. OTHER HOME HAD SIDING DAMAGE. DOZENS OF TALL HEALTHY TREES SNAPPED HALFWAY UP THE TRUNK. POWER POLES SNAPPED.
ROOF TORN OFF A HOUSE. TREES SHREDDED. TREE ON STRUCTURE.

EF-0 WITH PATH LENGTH ABOUT 3.6 MILES. BARN WALL PARTLY CAVED IN. HOUSE ATTACHMENT ON CINDERBLOCKS DESTROYED. TREES DAMAGED ALONG MUCH OF THE PATH. SECTION OF METAL ROOF TORN OFF BARN NEAR THE END OF THE PATH.
TWO TREES UPROOTED.
8.5 MILE PATH OF TREE DAMAGE EAST-NORTHEAST FROM THIS LOCATION. A MIX OF UPROOTS PLUS HEALTHY AND UNHEALTHY SNAPS.

A SECTION OF M DRIVE S NEAR 8 MILE AND K DRIVE S WEST OF M-311 HAD UP TO 90 MPH ESTIMATED WINDS IN THE FORM OF DOZENS OF TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. TOURS OF FIELDS WITH PROPERTY OWNERS REVEALED NO SIGN OF DEBRIS LOFTED OR DEPOSITED DOWNWIND.
AN ISOLATED AREA OF TREE DAMAGE NEAR M-311 AND D DRIVE S NOT CONNECTED WITH THE 8.5 MILE LONG WIND DAMAGE PATH A FEW MILES SOUTH OF THIS LOCATION.

EF0 TORNADO MOVED THROUGH NORTHERN PORTIONS OF ALLEGAN COUNTY FOR ABOUT 3 MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS ABOUT 100 YARDS. ONE OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED NEAR 26TH STREET WITH METAL ROOFING DEBRIS SCATTERED ALONG THE PATH. A LARGE IRRIGATION STRUCTURE WAS ALSO KNOCKED OVER AND DAMAGED EAST OF 26TH STREET. INTERMITTENT TREE AND SOME MINOR ROOFING DAMAGE WAS NOTED THROUGH THE PATH ON 24TH, 23RD, 22ND AND 21ST STREET. TORNADIC DAMAGE TO TREES AND WAS NOTED THROUGHOUT THE PATH THAT ENDED JUST EAST OF 21ST STREET. TIME AND LOCATION ARE FOR STARTING LOCATION OF TORNADO PATH.
ISOLATED SWATHS OF TREES DOWN ALONG 142ND AVE NEAR AND EAST OF 21ST ST. WAS A HALF-MILE SOUTH OF TORNADO PATH.
